_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in releasing serialized and time-sensitive reports
reference_document_element_description: Maintain Records of Processing Activities, including processing purposes, data categories, recipient categories, transfer records, retention periods, and technical and organizational security measures descriptions.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI RC domain. RC-T-006 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-T-004 (RC, cosine: 0.467), TF-S-002 (TF, cosine: 0.452).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.0/10 (raw 0.197). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [releasing, reports, sensitive, serialized, skill, time] vs WIDAI-only terms [activities, categories, data, descriptions, maintain, measures] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.854 (P=0.870, R=0.839). P exceeds R by 0.031 — the DCWF element's 7 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 23 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (98.2%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Skill in releasing serialized and time-sensitive reports" and "Maintain Records of Processing Activities, including processing purposes, data c". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.529 > STS 0.197 (gap +0.332). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 3.9%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [releasing, reports, sensitive, serialized, skill, time]. WIDAI terms: [activities, categories, data, descriptions, maintain, measures].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 98.2% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.332 with low STS 0.197. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
